==Pokémon==
Nando tends to choose Pokémon with some sort of musical ability which fits his musical style. He seems to favor {{t|Bug}}- and {{type|Grass}} Pokémon and he likes to use [[sound-based move]]s during his performances and battles. Nando's Pokémon are also known for being able to exhibit both beauty and strength.

This listing is of Nando's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:

===In rotation===
{{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}}
{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Roserade
|stage1=Budew
|stage2=Roselia
|type1=Grass
|type2=Poison
|img=Nando Roserade.png
|epnum=DP004
|epname=Dawn Of A New Era!
|vajp=Satsuki Yukino
|vaen=Sarah Natochenny
|desc={{p|Roserade}} appears to be Nando's main Pokémon. It first appeared as a {{p|Budew}} in ''[[DP004|Dawn Of A New Era!]]'', where it fought against both {{Ash}} and {{an|Dawn}}. It [[Evolution|evolved]] later into {{p|Roselia}} in the same episode. It appeared again in ''[[DP036|A Secret Sphere of Influence!]]'', where it was revealed that it had defeated [[Gardenia]]'s {{p|Turtwig}}. It also appeared in ''[[DP049|Dawn's Early Night!]]'' during the {{ci|Hearthome}} {{pkmn|Contest}}.

In ''[[DP176|Coming Full-Festival Circle!]]'', it helped Nando reach the semi-finals of the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]] alongside {{p|Sunflora}} and it was revealed that it had evolved into a Roserade.

In ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]'', during the [[Lily of the Valley Conference]], it battled against [[Ash's Staraptor]]. Early in the battle, it was able to overpower Staraptor. However, thanks to a powerful {{m|Brave Bird}}, Staraptor was able to defeat Roserade.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Sunflora
|type1=Grass
|img=Nando Sunflora.png
|epnum=DP036
|epname=A Secret Sphere of Influence!
|vajp=
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Sunflora}} first appeared in ''[[DP036|A Secret Sphere of Influence!]]'', where it ran off after Nando was accused of stealing the [[Adamant Orb]]. It proved Nando's innocence by tracking down {{TRT}}, who really stole the orb. It later appeared in ''[[DP049|Dawn's Early Night!]]'' during the {{ci|Hearthome}} {{pkmn|Contest}}.

It made brief appearances in ''[[DP175|Opposites Interact!]]'' and ''[[DP176|Coming Full-Festival Circle!]]'', where it had helped Nando get to the semi-finals alongside {{TP|Nando|Kricketune}} in another battle.

Sunflora's known moves are {{m|Leech Seed}}, {{m|Cut}}, and {{m|Grass Whistle}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Kricketune
|type1=Bug
|img=Nando Kricketune.png
|epnum=DP049
|epname=Dawn's Early Night! 
|vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi
|vaen=Katsuyuki Konishi 
|desc={{p|Kricketune}} first appeared in ''[[DP049|Dawn's Early Night!]]'', where it was used in the {{ci|Hearthome}} {{pkmn|Contest}} alongside {{p|Sunflora}} in a music-based appeal and [[Nando's Roserade|Roselia]] in battles. It later helped Nando win the {{to|Beach Rose}} Contest prior to ''[[DP106|A Breed Stampede!]]''.

During the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]], in ''[[DP176|Coming Full-Festival Circle!]]'', it and {{p|Lopunny}} were used in the semi-finals, where they lost to [[Zoey]]'s {{p|Mismagius}} and {{p|Leafeon}} on points.

In ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]'', during the [[Lily of the Valley Conference]], it faced [[Ash's Heracross]] as Nando's last Pokémon, and eventually lost the match.}}

|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Altaria

|type2=Flying
|img=Nando Altaria.png
|epnum=DP174

|desc={{p|Altaria}} is Nando's fourth known Pokémon. It was used in Nando's appeal in the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]] alongside his {{p|Kricketot}}.

Altaria's only known move is {{m|Perish Song}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Kricketot
|type1=Bug
|img=Nando Kricketot.png
|epnum=DP174
|epname=Last Call — First Round!
|vajp=
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Kricketot}} is Nando's fifth known Pokémon. It was used in Nando's appeal in the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]] alongside his {{p|Altaria}}. Using its antennae, Kricketot was able to create music that, when used in combination with Altaria's Perish Song, created a dazzling appeal that easily ensured Nando's progression past the first round.

Kricketot's only known move is {{m|Sing}}{{tt|*|Kricketot cannot learn Sing in the games.}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Lopunny
|type1=Normal
|img=Nando Lopunny.png
|epnum=DP176
|epname=Coming Full-Festival Circle!
|vajp=Ikue Otani
|vaen=
|desc={{p|Lopunny}} is Nando's sixth known Pokémon. It was used in the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]], where its dancing skills were well suited alongside {{TP|Nando|Kricketune}}'s ability to create music and were used to simulate a {{wp|ballerina}} and a {{wp|violinist}}, respectively. The two were able to put up a good fight against Zoey's {{p|Mismagius}} and {{p|Leafeon}} during the Semi-Finals. However, it wasn't enough against Zoey's highly trained Pokémon and the two had the fewest points by the time the clock ran out.

It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]''.

Lopunny's known moves are {{m|Jump Kick}}, {{m|Protect}}, {{m|Blizzard}}, and {{m|Focus Blast}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Nando
|pkmn=Armaldo
|type1=Rock
|type2=Bug
|img=Nando Armaldo.png
|epnum=DP183
|epname=League Unleashed!
|vajp=
|vaen=Billy Beach
|desc={{p|Armaldo}} is Nando's seventh known Pokémon. It was used against [[Ash's Staraptor]] and [[Ash's Quilava]] during the [[Lily of the Valley Conference]]. It managed to defeat Ash's weakened Staraptor and put up a good fight against his Quilava before they were both knocked out.

Armaldo's known moves are {{m|Protect}}, {{m|Rock Blast}}, and {{m|X-Scissor}}.}}
